summary: Removed an accidental debug print from tools/link.py inside edit_mk, preventing the link-editing tool from printing makefile content when src_str is already present.
what_changed: Deleted a single line, print(s), from the nested edit_mk function in tools/link.py.
The surrounding logic still returns None when src_str is already present and otherwise replaces asm_str with src_str.
smart_moves: Kept the fix minimal: only the unintended debug output was removed, with no behavior changes to the replacement logic.
Preserved the idempotent branch where edit_mk returns None if src_str is already present.
decomp_lessons: Tooling scripts used during decompilation should avoid stray stdout/debug prints because they can pollute command output, CI logs, or scripted workflows.
When cleaning up tooling, prefer a tiny targeted diff if the intended behavior is already correct except for diagnostic noise.
naming_conventions: No new naming conventions were introduced.
Existing local names visible in the diff include edit_file, edit_mk, src_str, and asm_str.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly or matching tactics were changed; this PR only removed debug output from a Python tooling script.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR context.
follow_up_queries: Check tools/link.py for other accidental debug output before relying on its stdout in automation.
Search for print statements in tools scripts that may be leftover diagnostics rather than user-facing output.
